what_it_does: This file adds an interpretive render layer for decision records: it selects a visual device shape for a decision, generates the corresponding HTML/SVG infographic, and derives a slide-content object for an explanatory presentation. It keeps render hints separate from the authored decision meaning stored elsewhere.
observations: Defines a HINT map keyed by decision id that names a device (hero-split, duo, checkMatrix, taxonomy, bars, ledger), diagram seeds, shape lines, and optional figure data.
decisionShape resolves a record's shape first from record.shape, then HINT, then a structural heuristic based on option count and recommended label.
decisionDevice delegates rendering to functions on DNA.org (hubNetwork, checkMatrix, bars, connector, taxonomyEmergence) and returns an HTML/SVG string or empty string if the renderer is absent.
decisionSlide builds a slide object with stage label, title, lead, figure, options list, and an icon-anchored reading-protocol point list.
Exposes DNA._decisionHints for external harnesses or future shape-field authoring.
Inline comments repeatedly emphasize calmness, a single diagram per card, no dashboard furniture, and registry-is-truth precedence.
standouts: The hero-split device renders five hub nodes as a shared-spine diagram for 'substrate-home'.
The duo device explicitly renders two opposed state plates, a connector joint labeled 'authorize', and a reversible axis below.
The checkMatrix device lights the rightmost 'Both'/'Mix' union column as the self/recommended column.
The bars device uses an ordinal ramp and marks the recommended bar via a CSS class driven by recIndex.
The taxonomy device shows provisional ghost kinds fading into real emerged kinds.
conventions: Uses an IIFE attached to the global window object and extends a shared DNA namespace.
Accesses renderer dependencies lazily through const org = () => DNA.org || {}.
Uses a switch statement keyed by resolved device name to branch rendering logic.
Keeps node labels and shape lines in the HINT map rather than deriving them from the record at runtime.
Returns empty strings as fallbacks when a renderer or hint is missing.
concerns: Strong runtime dependency on DNA.org renderers that are not defined in this file; missing renderers silently produce empty output.
The structural heuristic in decisionShape may misclassify decisions whose option counts or recommended labels match the simple patterns.
HINT data is hardcoded, so new decisions must be added to the map or carry an explicit record.shape.
The figure/focal is only populated for 'substrate-home'; other decisions get no figure even though the slot exists.
Inline bug/critique annotations (R2, R3) suggest prior layout issues but are not actionable outside the file context.
notes: A thin, data-driven render-hint layer that turns decision records into calm, single-diagram cards and explanatory slides without altering the underlying authored content.
questions: Where are the DNA.org renderer functions (hubNetwork, checkMatrix, bars, connector, taxonomyEmergence) defined and what contracts do they expect?
How is record.shape authored and validated in the upstream decision registry?
What consumes DNA.decisionSlide and how are the returned options and points laid out in the slide template?
Is the empty-string fallback for unmapped devices intentional, or should there be a default minimal renderer?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it is a render-hint layer that selects devices and derives slides while leaving authored meaning untouched.
apparent_themes: Separation of render hints from authored recollection content.
Reversibility and recommendation as first-class visual signals.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make decision cards feel crafted and varied without hand-building each card, by mapping each decision to a small family of reusable visual devices and a consistent slide structure.
novelty: It treats decision rendering as an interpretive layer over a generic rendering wire, using a per-id hint map plus structural heuristics to choose among several bespoke diagram types.
contribution: It contributes a centralized, extensible mapping from decision records to visual devices and slide content, allowing new decisions to inherit a calm, consistent presentation once a shape or hint is provided.
summary_for_embedding: decision-render.js is an interpretive render-hint module that selects a visual device shape for each decision, generates its HTML/SVG diagram via DNA.org renderers, and derives a structured slide object for explanatory presentations.
intention_for_embedding: The file was written to keep decision cards visually varied and craft-led while avoiding per-card hand-building and without touching the authored decision meaning in the content registry.

symbols.does: Returns the DNA.org object, or an empty object if unavailable, used to access shared renderer functions.
symbols.description: A lazy accessor defined as an arrow function that returns DNA.org || {}. It is called inside decisionDevice to reach pluggable renderers such as hubNetwork, checkMatrix, bars, connector, and taxonomyEmergence, keeping the dependency lookup deferred.

symbols.does: Resolves a decision record to a device name using registry value, hint map, or structural heuristic.
symbols.description: Checks record.shape first, then the HINT map for the record id, then falls back to heuristics based on option count and recommended label. It encodes the registry-is-truth rule and provides graceful classification for unhinted records.

symbols.does: Generates the HTML/SVG infographic string for a decision's visual zone.
symbols.description: Looks up the decision's hint and resolved shape, then switches through device implementations (hero-split, checkMatrix, bars, duo, taxonomy, ledger) to call the appropriate DNA.org renderer and assemble markup. Returns an empty string when no renderer or hint is available.

symbols.does: Builds a slide-content object that presents a decision's title, lead, figure, options, and reading protocol.
symbols.description: Composes a structured object for DNA.renderExplained from the record's legibility fields, the HINT shape line, the options list, and an icon-anchored point list. It moves the full option implications into the slide so the card can stay compact.
